question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 13?

fragility_points: Unexpected macro, regulatory, security, or liquidation event before settlement.
Binance-specific market or candle anomaly at the decisive minute.
key_assumptions: No major adverse macro or BTC-specific shock hits before noon ET.
Binance's settlement-relevant BTCUSDT candle feed remains operational and representative.
A >4% selloff into the specific settlement minute is possible but not the default base-rate path.
main_logical_chain: The market resolves on a narrow, explicit Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close rule.
Observed Binance spot during analysis was about 71.1k, materially above 68k.
Without a meaningful shock, outside-view odds favor remaining above a threshold that is more than 3k lower over the remaining window.
Therefore Yes is highly likely, though not certain because BTC is volatile and the resolution minute is narrow.
payload.main_thesis: BTC was trading around 71.1k during analysis, so a noon ET Binance 1-minute close above 68k remained the clear base-rate outcome absent a meaningful adverse shock.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified.

payload.source_quality_view: High-quality primary rules plus direct exchange verification; low source-of-truth ambiguity and medium evidence independence.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still drop more than 4% intraday before noon ET.
The contract resolves on a single 1-minute close, so minute-specific volatility or venue dislocation could matter.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance pricing during the run was around 71.1k, comfortably above the 68k threshold.
Polymarket rules are explicit that the only decisive condition is the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close being strictly above 68,000.
Secondary CoinGecko pricing was directionally consistent with Binance spot.
payload.timing_relevance: Very high: this is a same-day, noon-ET, single-minute candle-close contract.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact remaining time-to-settlement at final read affects how much room there is for a large move.
Single-minute settlement always leaves some tail risk even when spot is well above the threshold.
payload.what_would_change_view: A sharp move toward 69k or below, a major adverse headline, or evidence of Binance-specific settlement/candle issues would lower confidence materially.
